query: Current fill percentage/storage of Lázaro Cárdenas (El Palmito) dam in Durango Mexico from Conagua SINA, latest daily readings for July 2026 and late June 2026
answer:
I've completed the research on the Lázaro Cárdenas ("El Palmito") dam. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Latest Storage Readings

**July 2026 (most recent):** El Palmito had fallen to **21% of capacity** as of ~July 8, 2026 (Conagua hydrometric report via Milenio), with inflows of just 44 m³/s and outflows of 109 m³/s to the Las Tórtolas regulating dam for the 2026 agricultural cycle.

**Late June/early July 2026:** **765 Mm³ = 25.87%** of capacity, declining ~7 Mm³/day (El Coahuilense, weekly Conagua stats).

**June 1, 2026 (official Conagua boletín):** **915.948 Mm³ = 31.0%** full — the highest-volume dam in Durango. Statewide, Durango's 10 main dams were at 36.2% (1,416.670 of 3,911.463 Mm³).

## Trajectory
The dam is in a **steep seasonal drawdown**: ~31% (June 1) → ~25.87% (late June) → 21% (July 8), driven by extractions exceeding weak rainfall inflows. Gates were expected to close in late July 2026, potentially ending around 565 Mm³. For historical context, it peaked at ~45% in October 2025 and hit lows of 17.1% (July 2025) and 18.47% (July 2024).

**Data limitation:** The official Conagua SINA portal (`sinav30.conagua.gob.mx:8080/Presas/`) is a JavaScript app that couldn't be scraped directly, so all figures come from news outlets explicitly citing Conagua/SINA daily and weekly reports.

sources.title: Presa El Palmito al 21 por ciento; escasas aportaciones agravan crisis hídrica

sources.title: Monitoreo de Presas - Conagua SINA
sources.snippet: Official Conagua SINA dam monitoring portal (JavaScript app; not directly scrapable).

key_facts: As of ~July 8, 2026, El Palmito was at 21% of capacity per Conagua's hydrometric report, with 44 m³/s inflow and 109 m³/s outflow to the Las Tórtolas regulating dam.
In late June/early July 2026, El Palmito held 765 Mm³ = 25.87% of capacity, declining roughly 7 Mm³ per day due to agricultural-cycle extractions (El Coahuilense, citing weekly Conagua data).
On June 1, 2026 (official Conagua boletín), El Palmito held 915.948 Mm³ = 31.0% full — the highest-volume dam in Durango.
Durango's 10 main dams collectively held 1,416.670 Mm³ = 36.2% of total capacity (3,911.463 Mm³) at the start of June 2026.
The dam was expected to close its gates in the last week of July 2026, potentially ending the 2026 cycle around 565 Mm³.
Historical context: ~45% in Oct 2025, ~25% Aug 2025, 17.1% low on July 3, 2025, and 18.47% historic low in July 2024.
El Palmito is Durango's largest dam, in Irrigation District 017, feeding the Comarca Lagunera and the Agua Saludable para La Laguna project.
The July 2026 21% level represents a sharp seasonal drawdown from ~31% at the start of June 2026; rainfall in the upper Nazas basin has been insufficient for recovery.
